mnemonics.title: MUX SELECTION
mnemonics.content: MUX = MANY INPUTS, ONE OUTPUT, CONTROLLED BY SELECT LINES. REMEMBER: MULTIPLE INPUTS, eXclusive selection.

mnemonics.title: SELECT LINE COUNT
mnemonics.content: SELECT LINES FORMULA: n = log2(N), WHERE N = NUMBER OF INPUTS. SAY: TO SELECT FROM 8, NEED 3 BITS (2^3 = 8).

acronyms.expansion: Multiplexer - Many inputs to One output
acronyms.usage: Used in data routing and selection applications

acronyms.expansion: Demultiplexer - One input to Many outputs

acronyms.usage: Controls when MUX is active or disabled
Control lines that choose which input passes to output

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a rotary switch with multiple positions. The select lines are like the knob position that connects to different input contacts. As you rotate (change select lines), different inputs connect to the single output.

visualTips.concept: MUX Truth Table as Routing Map
visualTips.visualization: Think of select lines as addresses. S1S0 = 00 sends you to input 0, 01 to input 1, 10 to input 2, 11 to input 3. The output is whatever arrives at that address.
Each AND gate is like a programmable path. The select line combination activates exactly one path at a time, like railroad switches directing a train to one track.

analogies.analogy: A MUX is like a single-channel TV remote that switches between different input sources (DVD, USB, HDMI) based on which button you press - only one source plays at a time.
Select lines work like a postal sorting system. The postal code (select lines) determines which sorting bin (input) gets routed to the delivery truck (output).
Enable input is like a power switch on an amplifier - when off (disabled), no sound comes out regardless of which input is selected.
